info: Forest cover has fallen far below the Atlantic peak, replaced by a managed cultural landscape — closing a 23-million-year thread of herbivore-shaped open woodland.
background: The cultural landscape. Closed-canopy forest cover has been dramatically reduced from the Atlantic peak, but what replaced it — farmland, managed woodland, urban areas — bears no resemblance to the natural mosaic woodland baseline either. The closest surviving analogues to the pre-megafauna natural vegetation structure are found in traditionally grazed wood-pasture systems in parts of eastern Europe, Iberia's dehesa landscapes, and active trophic rewilding sites. Across the map area as a whole, perhaps 30–35% is wooded, but almost entirely managed, fragmented and low in large-herbivore biomass. The 23-million-year thread of open woodland mosaic maintained by large herbivores has been effectively severed.
